title: How to Get a SORN in the UK: Your Quick & Easy DVLA Guide
metaDescription: Need to declare your car off-road? Learn exactly how to get a SORN in the UK with our simple DVLA guide. Discover who needs one, how to apply, and key rules to follow.
targetKeyword: how to get a sorn in the uk
content:
To get a SORN (Statutory Off Road Notification) in the UK, you must tell the DVLA that your vehicle is officially off the road. This means it will not be driven or parked on public roads. The easiest way is online via GOV.UK, using your V11 tax reminder or V5C logbook. You can also apply by phone or post. Declaring a SORN ensures you avoid fines for untaxed cars kept on private land.

## What is a SORN, Anyway?

A SORN, or Statutory Off Road Notification, is how you officially tell the DVLA your vehicle is off the road. This means it won't be driven or parked on any public road. Instead, it’ll be kept strictly on private land. Think of your driveway, garage, or a private field. It's a legal requirement if your car isn't taxed and insured for public use in the UK.

## Why You Need to SORN Your Car

Declaring a SORN is crucial for several common reasons. If your car isn't taxed, it must have a SORN. This applies even if it's just sitting idle in your garage. Without it, you could face hefty fines and legal issues.

Here are the main reasons to SORN your vehicle:

*   **Not Roadworthy:** Your car failed its MOT, or it's a non-runner. You need time to fix it before it can legally be on the road.
*   **Private Project:** You're restoring a classic, or keeping a spare vehicle on private land. You don’t intend to drive it for a while.
*   **Written Off:** Your insurer declared it a 'write-off'. It's no longer safe or legal for the road.
*   **Exporting:** You're taking the car permanently out of the UK.
*   **Scrapping:** If you’re sending your car to the scrap heap, a SORN is needed until it’s officially destroyed. We can help you [sell your car for scrap at BuyAndScrap.com](/sell) easily.

### The Golden Rule: Tax It or SORN It!

Remember this key rule: every car registered in the UK must either be taxed or have a SORN. There's no middle ground. If your car is untaxed and without a SORN, even on private land, you face fines, penalties, or even court action. Avoid the hassle – get it sorted!

## How to Get a SORN in the UK: Your Step-by-Step Guide

Getting a SORN in the UK is straightforward. It usually takes just a few minutes. Here’s how you can declare your car off the road:

### 1. Apply Online (The Easiest Way)

This is the fastest and most popular method. You'll need one of these documents handy:

*   **Your V11 reminder letter:** Find the 16-digit number on the letter you get when your car tax is due.
*   **Your V5C logbook (registration document):** Use the 11-digit document reference number from the front page.

Once you have a number, visit the [GOV.UK website](https://www.gov.uk/make-a-sorn). Search for 'SORN' and follow the simple instructions. Your SORN can start immediately or on the first day of the next month.

### 2. By Phone

Prefer to speak to someone? Call the DVLA vehicle enquiries line. You'll need either your 16-digit V11 reminder number or your 11-digit V5C logbook number ready.

### 3. By Post

If you prefer traditional mail, complete a V890 form (Statutory Off Road Notification). You can print this form from GOV.UK or get one from a Post Office that handles vehicle tax. Send the completed form to: DVLA, Swansea, SA99 1AR. Your SORN will start on the date you specify on the form.

## What Happens After You SORN Your Car?

Once you've declared a SORN, there are a few important things to remember. Your car must be kept on private land. This means no parking on public roads, even outside your house. Breaking this rule can lead to significant fines.

### Re-taxing Your Car

If you decide to drive your car again, you'll need to tax it first. Your SORN automatically ends when you pay for vehicle tax. Remember, you'll also need valid insurance and an up-to-date MOT to drive on public roads. You can check your car's MOT status for free with our [Free MOT Checker](/mot-checker).

### Selling a SORN Car

You can sell a car with a SORN in place. When you sell it, the SORN doesn't transfer to the new owner. They will need to either tax the vehicle or apply for a new SORN themselves. If you're looking to sell your car quickly, [BuyAndScrap.com can help](/sell).

## Frequently Asked Questions about SORNs

**Q: Can I drive a SORN car to an MOT test?**

A: Yes, you can. You are allowed to drive a SORN car directly to and from a pre-booked MOT test. However, the car must still be insured for the journey.

**Q: How long does a SORN last?**

A: A SORN lasts indefinitely until you tax your vehicle again, sell it, or scrap it. You do not need to renew it annually.

**Q: What if I move house with a SORN car?**

A: You must update your address with the DVLA. Your SORN remains valid, but the car must still be kept on private land at your new address.

Whether you’re ready to get back on the road or need to declare your vehicle off-road, understanding SORN rules is key. If you're looking for your next honest, affordable car, why not [Browse Cars](/cars) available at BuyAndScrap.com today?
